_meta.note: AI-authored outcome rules. Outcomes represent security program capabilities that emerge from combining multiple tools — not single-vendor claims. Hover cards show which stack modules contribute.

families.name: Identify
families.description: Know your assets, exposures, and risk posture

families.name: Protect
families.description: Enforce controls that reduce attack surface

families.name: Detect
families.description: Find threats and anomalies before they escalate

families.name: Respond
families.description: Contain incidents and recover with minimal impact

families.name: Program
families.description: Cross-cutting program capabilities and emerging requirements

outcomes.name: Continuous Asset Risk Inventory
outcomes.description: All assets — on-prem, cloud, SaaS, external — are continuously discovered, classified, and risk-scored. The security team has a live, prioritised view of the attack surface.

outcomes.name: Vulnerability Risk Reduction
outcomes.description: Vulnerabilities are discovered, risk-ranked, and remediated before attackers exploit them. Patch SLAs are enforced and deviation tracked.

outcomes.name: Zero Trust Network Access
outcomes.description: No implicit trust for any user, device, or location. Every access request is verified against identity, device posture, and risk signals before a minimal-privilege session is granted.

outcomes.name: Privileged Account Lifecycle
outcomes.description: Privileged credentials are vaulted, rotated automatically, and dispensed just-in-time. All privileged sessions are recorded. Lateral movement via standing privilege is eliminated.

outcomes.name: IGA Access Certification
outcomes.description: User entitlements are continuously reviewed and certified by business owners. Orphaned accounts, excessive permissions, and SoD violations are surfaced and remediated on a scheduled cadence.

outcomes.name: Email-Borne Threat Defense
outcomes.description: Phishing, BEC, and malware delivered via email are blocked before reaching users. Domain authentication (DMARC/SPF/DKIM) prevents spoofing. Suspicious URLs are rewritten and sandboxed.

outcomes.name: Data Loss Prevention
outcomes.description: Sensitive data — PII, IP, financial records — is discovered, classified, and protected from exfiltration via email, web, cloud sync, and removable media. Insider threat activity is monitored.

outcomes.name: Continuous Controls Monitoring
outcomes.description: Security controls are continuously validated against policy baselines. Deviations are automatically detected, logged, and routed for remediation — not discovered during annual audits.

outcomes.name: Cloud Security Posture Management
outcomes.description: Cloud infrastructure misconfigurations, over-permissioned IAM roles, exposed storage buckets, and unpatched workloads are continuously detected across IaaS, PaaS, and container environments.

outcomes.name: Endpoint Detection & Response
outcomes.description: Malicious behaviour on endpoints is detected using behavioural telemetry — not just signatures. Threats are automatically contained, investigated, and remediated with full attack-chain visibility.

outcomes.name: Managed Detection & Response
outcomes.description: 24/7 human-led threat hunting, alert triage, and incident response — extending the in-house SOC. MDR analysts investigate, contain, and provide post-incident reports with actionable recommendations.

outcomes.name: Credential Compromise Response
outcomes.description: When credentials are stolen or breached, the security stack detects the event, invalidates sessions, forces rotation, and limits blast radius — all within minutes, not days.

outcomes.name: Ransomware Resilience
outcomes.description: The organisation can detect a ransomware intrusion early, contain it, and recover to a known-good state from immutable backups — limiting business disruption to hours, not weeks.

outcomes.name: DevSecOps Pipeline Security
outcomes.description: Security is integrated into the CI/CD pipeline — secrets are never committed, dependencies are scanned for CVEs, and code is analysed for vulnerabilities before reaching production.

outcomes.name: Agentic AI Governance
outcomes.description: AI agents with tool-use capabilities introduce novel risks: credential access, prompt injection, and autonomous lateral movement. No current stack tool addresses model-level access controls, tool-use auditing, or agent identity governance. This outcome is not yet achievable with today's vendor landscape.
